nodes.text: Hi, um — I just came from urgent care. They gave me amoxicillin. I've never had to take antibiotics before so I was hoping someone here could — is this a good time to ask questions? I don't want to slow things down.

responses.text: You can always ask questions. What's on your mind?
Let me get your prescription going first, then we can talk.

nodes.text: Okay, great. So — is it okay to take it with food? I read online that some antibiotics you're not supposed to, but I think amoxicillin might be okay? And is it normal to feel worse before you feel better?

responses.text: Amoxicillin is fine with food, actually recommended. And yes, a day or two of feeling worse is normal.
Your doctor would have told you if there were food restrictions. What's your concern about feeling worse?

nodes.text: Yeah, sure, that makes sense. Theo Marsh. M-A-R-S-H. The prescription is from the urgent care on Fourth — Dr. Reyes, I think. I still have the wristband on, sorry.

responses.text: I can see that. It's fine. Can I see your ID?

nodes.text: That's such a relief. And — this is maybe a dumb question — but do I have to finish the whole course even if I feel better? My roommate didn't finish his last month and he seemed fine, but my doctor was pretty specific about it.

responses.text: Finish the course. Your doctor's right. Stopping early lets resistant bacteria survive.
Your roommate got lucky. Your doctor's right — finish it.

nodes.text: My throat is really bad and I've had a headache all day. I just want to know if that's strep being strep or if something else is going on.

responses.text: That's strep being strep. The amoxicillin will start working in twenty-four to forty-eight hours.

nodes.text: Okay, that's really helpful. Oh — is it weird that my roommate had the same prescription last month? Like the exact same drug and dose? We live in the same apartment. Is strep going around or something?

responses.text: Strep is contagious, so yes, it's possible it went between you two.
Not weird at all. Can I see your ID to get this filled?

nodes.text: Oh right, yeah. Here. Sorry, my hands are a little clammy — I've been running a fever. They said it would come down once the antibiotic kicks in.
